body.text: RTB · 2A WEEKLY INTEL · Week of July 13 – July 17, 2026
Countdown week: Virginia holds its July 21 date, Congress finally scores — the House passes H.R. 1181, 221–201 — and the NFA registry fight opens a civil flank in four courts at once.

The week's defining feature was a calendar, not a crisis. Virginia's statewide injunction held toward July 21 as dealers queued held orders, while the courts kept producing: Barnett reframed, DOJ's California TRO denied on §12601 standing, and the NFA registry attack went four-court.

facts.title: 3. Barnett v. Raoul — reframed, then cooled

body.text:
• [Calendar] Jul 21 — Virginia statewide injunction takes effect
• [Calendar] Aug 4 — ATF EITB comment window closes
• [Carry-over] H.R. 1181 heads to the Senate — S. 1715 hearing watch
• [Predicted] July NICS shows a VA long-gun spike; NFA surge holds
